scenarios.prompt: I have a JVM-only Spring Boot project. Show me the minimal Gradle change to add SKaiNET so I can build a small MLP and run inference.
scenarios.must_not_contain_reason: Hard rule 2: BOM is sk.ainet, libraries are sk.ainet.core; hard rule 1: never hard-pin per-artifact versions when the BOM is in use.

scenarios.prompt: I want to load a Llama-style GGUF model. What do I add on top of the minimal setup?
scenarios.must_not_contain_reason: GGUF has a dedicated loader; pulling ONNX or SafeTensors only adds transitive weight.

scenarios.prompt: Add SKaiNET to a KMP module that targets jvm() and androidTarget(). The DSL should be usable from commonMain.
I want the latest 0.20.0-SNAPSHOT. Configure my repositories.
scenarios.must_not_contain_reason: Snapshot artifacts come from the public snapshot repo, not mavenLocal.

scenarios.prompt: My consumer project already uses gradle/libs.versions.toml. Show me the version-catalog entries for SKaiNET.
scenarios.must_not_contain_reason: Per-library `version.ref` is fine on the BOM line itself, but library entries (skainet-lang-core etc.) should NOT carry a version because the BOM constrains them.
